So M2.7 is the AI brain inside Maxclaw. And here's the wild part. M2.7 is Miniax's first model that deeply participates in its own evolution. That means the AI helped build itself like a student who writes her own textbooks and then grades her own tests and improves based on that. Now, Miniaax actually used earlier versions of the model to build a research agent harness capable of managing D pipelines, training environments and evaluation infrastructure. The AI ran its own experiments. Essentially, it fixed its own mistakes. It improved in loops itself of 100 rounds without any human telling it what to do. And according to internal benchmarks, M2.7 handled approximately 30 to 50% of its own development workflow autonomously, monitoring experiments, analyzing logs, and implementing code repairs. This has never been seen at this level. It's kind of a big deal. So, if you're wondering how does it work and how good is it? So, you can see the benchmarks right here on the S. Pro benchmark, which tests real world coding ability. M2.7 scored 56.22% nearly approaching the best level of anthropics Opus models. That's massive for a model at this price point. In open usage specifically, M2.7 shows significant improvement over M2.5, approaching the latest Sonic 4.6 on MM Claw evaluation. The translation, it's nearly as smart as one of the best AR models in the world, but it costs way less, right? M2.7 achieves a 97% skill adherence rate across 40 complex skills. That means when you tell it to do something complicated, it does it correctly 97 times out of 100. It literally hasn't failed on me yet. I haven't run 100 experiments on it, but I can tell you that it tends to work pretty much first time round on almost everything that I throw at it, which is pretty amazing. If you're like a very techy developer and you want a lot of customization, this probably isn't the right thing for you. And so it's quite easy to build stuff, deploy stuff, create stuff, generate images, generate media, and it's super simple and easy to go with. Right now, the catch is with Maxclaw is that it's locked to Miniax M2.7, right? So you can't switch to claude. You can't switch to GBT or Gemini or any local model. The model is fixed by the platform and all processing and memory storage happens on Minias's infrastructure. So that's kind of the downside of it. I would say if you want something like OpenClaw, that's really the full control option. So this is for like you know power users or developers or privacy conscious people right and the setup time is you know I would be looking at like 30 60 minutes minimum if you've never done this before now open core is also model agnostic that's the difference between miniax maxclaw and open core so you can run core GBT gemini deepsee or local models via alarm swap them between in one config line and you know you could actually have multiple models running at the same time with sub agents via open core as well plus all data is stored locally on your machine by default if you use local models via a llama then nothing leaves your computer right so you get a lot of control over that but the catch is you have to set up yourself you're responsible for updates security patches maintenance it's not plug-andplay so this is the difference between them this is how they work with you can see how powerful they are and you can see how easy it is to build stuff with this right so if we go back to max floor here's the other cool thing I can just close this tab and you know if I have to go out or if I have to go and do something I know this is just going to get done in the background whilst I'm speaking to you so for example here it's already setting up the react project set up and then it'll create the whole website for us, right? And then in the preview once it's ready to go, we've got that good to go. Same with the research, right? You just went off and researched this. This is using something that's just at the same level as something like Opus 4.6. So, it's really, really powerful, easy to use. The difference is I would say, you know, some people say, which one should you use? So I would pick Maxclaw if you want an agent running today with zero headaches, right? You're not technical. You live in Telegram, WhatsApp or Slack. You're okay with your data being on Minimax service. That's what I would go with. Pick openclaw if you want to run claw GPT or a local model. Maybe you care deeply about data privacy. Maybe you're technical enough to handle the setup. You want to mix and match models for different tasks. Then I would go with open claw. The honest truth is the best way to think about Miniax M2.7 plus Open Core is not as another chatbot combo. It's more like a new operating layer for personal productivity. Max Claw just got a massive brain upgrade and Open Claw still gives you the ultimate control and both are genuinely good right now. It's just the difference in terms of what you want.
